sourceMovie.title: Only Yesterday
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Only Yesterday (1991) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Isao Takahata's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Garden of Words
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Only Yesterday clicked for you, The Garden of Words (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Only Yesterday?
faq.answer: Try The Garden of Words, Ocean Waves, The Anthem of the Heart—each shares DNA with Only Yesterday while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Only Yesterday?
faq.answer: Only Yesterday (1991) is best described as Animation, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Only Yesterday worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
